news.summary: In Claremont, North Carolina, Wyre has chosen CommScope's cloud-native vCCAP Evo™ solution for Remote PHY (R-PHY) deployments. CommScope (NASDAQ: COMM) made the announcement, stating that their solution was selected for the R-PHY deployments.

news.summary: In Dallas, Texas, Copart, Inc. (NASDAQ: CPRT) released its financial results for the quarter ending on April 30, 2025. During this period, the company generated $1.2 billion in revenue, $552.3 million in gross profit, and $406.6 million in net income attributable to Copart, Inc. These figures signify a revenue growth of $84.5 million, equivalent to a 7.5% increase; a gross profit increase of $26.8 million, or a 5.1% rise; and a net income increase of $24.3 million.
news.headline: Copart reported an increase in revenue to $1.2 billion, with gross profit of $552.3 million and net income of $406.6 million.

news.summary: In the recent data released, it was reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the past week, with a total of 10,000 new cases recorded. This marks a 20% rise compared to the previous week. Additionally, the number of hospitalizations has also seen a surge, with 500 new admissions reported. The positivity rate has climbed to 5%, indicating a worrying trend of the virus spreading rapidly within the community. Authorities are urging the public to adhere to safety measures such as wearing masks and practicing social distancing to curb the further spread of the virus.

news.summary: HONG KONG, May 22, 2025—Everbright Digital Holdings Limited, a Hong Kong-based integrated marketing solutions provider, revealed the issuance of 160,000 ordinary shares at US$4.00 each, generating about $640,000 in gross proceeds. This came from the underwriter’s partial exercising of its over-allotment option following Everbright’s IPO announcement. The option closed on May 22, 2025. The company’s ordinary shares, trading under “EDHL” on Nasdaq Capital Market since April 17, 2025, saw this latest development.
news.headline: Everbright Digital Holdings issued 160,000 Ordinary Shares at $4.00 per share, raising $640,000 through partial exercise of over-allotment option.

news.summary: In Fort Worth, Texas, TXO Partners, L.P. (NYSE: TXO) disclosed a dual listing of its common units on NYSE Texas Inc., a recently established fully electronic equities exchange based in Dallas, TX. This move will see TXO maintaining its primary listing on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) and trading under the same ticker symbol, "TXO," on NYSE Texas. The founders of TXO have had a longstanding relationship with the NYSE dating back to 1980, spanning six listed companies.

news.summary: In Louisville, Kentucky, Brown-Forman Corporation (NYSE: BFA, BFB) disclosed that their Board of Directors has approved a standard quarterly cash dividend of $0.2265 per share for both their Class A and Class B Common Stock. The dividend is slated to be disbursed on July 1, 2025, to shareholders listed on the records by June 9, 2025. Being part of the renowned S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats index, Brown-Forman has a track record of issuing regular quarterly cash dividends for 81 years, and consecutively increasing them for 41 years.
news.headline: Brown-Forman (NYSE: BFA, BFB) declares $0.2265 per share dividend, payable on July 1, 2025, to stockholders on record by June 9, 2025.

news.summary: In Dublin, California, Ross Stores, Inc. announced its financial results for the 13-week period ending May 3, 2025. Earnings per share stood at $1.47, a slight increase from the $1.46 reported for the same period in 2024. The net income for this quarter was $479 million, a decrease from the $488 million recorded in the previous year. Sales for the first quarter of 2025 reached $5.0 billion, with comparable store sales remaining flat compared to last year. CEO Jim Conroy noted the challenges faced during the spring selling season but remained optimistic about the company's performance.
news.headline: Ross Stores reported Q1 EPS of $1.47 (up from $1.46 in 2024), net income of $479M (down from $488M) and sales of $5.0 billion with flat comp store sales compared to last year.

news.summary: GOLETA, California - Deckers Brands, a prominent company in innovative footwear, apparel, and accessories, has appointed Cynthia (Cindy) L. Davis as the Chair of the Board of Directors, replacing Michael (Mike) F. Devine, III, who served for over 14 years, including six years as Chair. This change is effective immediately. Cynthia Davis joined the Board in 2018. Deckers Brands is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol DECK.
news.headline: Deckers Brands appoints Cynthia L. Davis as new Chair of the Board, replacing Michael Devine after 14 years.

The agreement leverages QIAGEN's worldwide influence and automation proficiency alongside ID Solutions' expertise in assay development and manufacturing.

news.summary: Cavco Industries, Inc. reported financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year ending March 29, 2025. Quarterly net revenue was $508 million, up 21% from the previous year, with gross profit at 22.8%. Net income was $36 million and adjusted net income was $44 million. For the full fiscal year, net revenue was $2.015 billion, up 12.3%, with income before taxes at $211 million. Backlogs at March 29, 2025, were $197 million, up from the previous year. Stock repurchases were approximately $150 million in the year, with an additional $150 million approved. President and CEO, Bill Boor, mentioned a significant increase in activity in March and improvements in their go-to-market strategy by unifying manufacturing facilities under the Cavco name. Adjusted net income excludes a $10 million charge related to abandoned assets. Quarterly factory-built housing revenue saw a 22.4% increase in net revenue. Quarterly gross profit increased by 21.6%. Income increased by 11.3% in the factory-built housing segment. Net income per share was $4.47 and adjusted net income per share was $5.40 in the fourth quarter. For the full fiscal year, net factory-built housing revenue saw a 12.6% increase with increased modules and homes sold. Financial services segment net revenue decreased. Gross profit decreased in the financial services segment due to reduced revenue from loan sales. Adjusted net income for the full year was $21.63.
news.headline: Cavco Industries reports $508 million in net revenue for Q4; $2.015 billion for fiscal year. Net income at $36 million.

news.summary: Lionsgate Studios Corp reported its fourth quarter results for the quarter ended March 31, 2025, with revenue reaching $1.1 billion, a 22% increase year-over-year. Net income attributable to shareholders was $21.9 million or $0.10 diluted earnings per share, while adjusted net income was $60.9 million or $0.21 adjusted diluted earnings per share. Operating income significantly increased to $94.2 million, and adjusted OIBDA was up 49% to $138.3 million. Motion Picture segment profit and Studio Adjusted OIBDA reached the highest levels in 10 years. Trailing 12-month library revenue grew 8% to a record high of $956 million. The Motion Picture segment revenue grew 28% to $526.4 million, with segment profit up 65% to $135.3 million, driven by box office success, increased content deliveries, library demand, and lower spend. Television Production segment revenue increased by 16% to $543.3 million, while segment profit decreased to $40.6 million due to a tough comparison from the previous year. Lionsgate's senior management expressed satisfaction with the results given the challenging operating environment and looks forward to creating significant value as a standalone studio. The company will host an analyst and investor conference call on May 22nd to discuss the results.
news.headline: Lionsgate's Q4 Fiscal 2025 revenue: $1.1B, +22% YoY. Net Income: $21.9M, $0.10 EPS. Adjusted Net Income: $60.9M, $0.21 EPS. Oper. Income: $94.2M, up to $138.3M Adjusted OIBDA. Motion Picture Segment Profit highest in 10 years at $135.3M, Revenue: $526.4M. TV Production Segment Revenue: $543.3M, Profit: $40.6M. Library revenue in Q4: $340M, record $956M Trailing 12-month library revenue. Live webcast on May 22nd. CEO: Strong quarter despite challenges.

news.summary: Summary:ePlus Inc. reported financial results for Q4 and full year Fiscal 2025. In Q4, net sales decreased by 10.2% to $498.1 million, with Technology business net sales declining by 10.4% to $487.2 million, but service revenues increasing by 33.0% to $104.9 million. Gross profit for the quarter rose by 11.8% to $145.8 million, with a gross margin of 29.3%. Net earnings increased by 14.6% to $25.2 million, and adjusted EBITDA increased by 19.1% to $43.8 million. Diluted earnings per share increased by 15.9% to $0.95.For the full fiscal year 2025, net sales decreased by 7.0% to $2,068.8 million, with Technology business net sales declining by 7.7% to $2,009.1 million, but service revenues increasing by 37.1% to $400.4 million. Gross profit increased by 3.3% to $569.1 million, with a gross margin of 27.5%. However, net earnings decreased by 6.7% to $108.0 million, and adjusted EBITDA decreased by 6.4% to $178.2 million. Diluted earnings per share decreased by 6.5% to $4.05.Balance sheet highlights include cash and cash equivalents of $389.4 million as of March 31, 2025, a decrease in inventory by 13.8% to $120.4 million, and a decrease in accounts receivable by 19.8% to $517.1 million. Total stockholders' equity was $977.6 million.The company announced fiscal year 2026 guidance with low single-digit net sales growth, and mid single-digit growth for gross profit and adjusted EBITDA. They emphasized their focus on customer relationships, service and product offerings, and growth sectors like AI, cloud, security, and networking to drive profitability and shareholder value.CEO, Mark Marron, expressed confidence in the company's strategic priorities and ability to navigate uncertainties in the macro environment for fiscal 2026. They also discussed recent corporate achievements and recognitions. For more information, ePlus will hold a conference call on May 22, 2025.Disclaimer: The information provided above is a summarized version of the original news article sourced from PRNewswire. For comprehensive details, refer to the full story available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: ePlus financial report for 2025: Q4 net sales $498.1M, net earnings $25.2M, FY net sales $2,068.8M, net earnings $108.0M. EPS $0.95 in Q4 and $4.05 for the year. Gross margin Q4 29.3%, FY 27.5%. Adjusted EBITDA Q4 $43.8M, FY $178.2M. Cash equivalents $389.4M. Total stockholders' equity $977.6M. Guidance: low single digit net sales growth for FY 2026. More about ePlus on their website.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the unemployment rate in the country has reached 7.2%, with a total of 10.5 million individuals currently without a job. The government has stated that it plans to implement various measures to address this issue and stimulate job growth. Additionally, the report highlighted that the youth unemployment rate is at 15.3%, raising concerns about the future employment prospects for young people. The government is also aiming to focus on creating more opportunities for this demographic. Overall, the data underscores the challenges faced by the labor market and the importance of effective strategies to combat unemployment.

news.summary: Codere Online Luxembourg, S.A. has received a delisting notification from Nasdaq due to its failure to file the 2024 Form 20-F by December 31, 2024. The company engaged a new accounting firm on December 31, 2024, leading to delays in finalizing the financial audit. The Company filed the 2023 Form 20-F with the SEC on May 1, 2025. The Nasdaq Staff has determined to delist the Company's securities as a result of the missed filing, in accordance with the Public Reports Rule under Listing Rule 5250(c)(1). This development puts the future of Codere Online on a precarious edge.
news.headline: Codere Online faces delisting from Nasdaq due to failure to file 2024 Form 20-F; delays due to new audit firm.
